source_reviews.notes: Reopened official Census SUSB page. It identifies the 2022 tables, their April 2025 release, and firm, employment, payroll, and receipts measures at detailed NAICS levels. It supports the frozen population bridge, not individual-firm EBITDA or service mix.

Reopened official Census archive. Its 541930 definition supports the translation, interpreting, and sign-language boundary, but the page identifies itself as the 1997 NAICS archive rather than the frozen source metadata's stated 2017 vintage.

Reopened the seven-page Census NAPCS product list. It separately defines translation/localization and live interpretation products under 541930, supporting the frozen model taxonomy rather than count endpoints.

Reopened the provider ranking and just-below-ranking table. The cited U.S. cohort, reported revenues, service labels, and verified/estimated markers reproduce the frozen 7:6:1 observed composition. The artifact correctly treats the resulting counts as a non-random proxy, not observed NAICS/EBITDA counts.

Reopened ATA's review of the ALC 2015 survey. It reports the broad U.S. company mix of translation and interpreting revenue and supports both archetypes being material; it is correctly not treated as firm-count or target-band evidence.

Reopened the ranking and checked the base cohort and both endpoint constructions. The 7 observed translation/localization-led providers, 14-provider base after scaling, and 21-provider conservative high are coherent only as the declared LOW-quality proxy; the source does not establish firm NAICS or EBITDA.

Reopened the official SUSB table page. It supports the frozen 28-firm denominator's population source and detailed-industry data availability; the record honestly leaves its EBITDA-band bridge as ASSUMPTION/NONE.

Reopened. The historical survey supports only the packet's contextual statements about freelancer prevalence and quality review; it supplies no target cash-cost, pass-through, or realization primitive.

Reopened official DOJ guidance. It supports only the contextual statement that some covered communication requires effective, qualified interpreting and does not measure translation/localization survival, savings, or demand.

input_reviews.notes: Checked 6/12/20. The base scales the six observed providers to the 28-firm band; the high preserves the minimum seven translation-led and one residual firm. Overlap is disclosed and readiness is UNPROVEN.
Checked 7/14/21. The base scales the seven observed providers to the 28-firm band; the high preserves the minimum six interpreting-led and one residual firm. The proxy limitations are explicit.
Checked 0.7857/0.9286/0.9643 against the residual count range: (28-6)/28, (28-2)/28, and (28-1)/28, rounded to four decimals.
Checked 1/2/6 and its disjoint definition for balanced, other-service-led, or unclassifiable providers. It reconciles the enumeration-coverage endpoints without an industry-wide claim.
The frozen translation-localization-led ID follows the largest base count, 14 versus 12. Range overlap is retained as selection uncertainty and does not create a second primary record.
Reviewed null low/base/high. MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE with no source is honest because no closed target comparable supports an all-in entry multiple; no price is inferred from ranking or M&A intent.
Reviewed null low/base/high. Missingness is honest, and conditional buyer access/win remains separate from sale availability, paid price, and capacities.
Year-one low/base/high are all null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. No unsupported repricing, volume, mix, renewal, switching, implementation, or demand effect enters C.
Year-two low/base/high are all null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. Commercial retention is not backfilled from market-level technology or price-pressure commentary.
Year-three low/base/high are all null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. The packet preserves causal separation and makes no unsupported base or bounded range.
Year-four low/base/high are all null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. No implementation investment or operator-controlled survival is embedded in C.
Year-five low/base/high are all null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. The absence of target retention evidence correctly propagates to an indeterminate base result.
Reviewed null low/base/high and null cost ID. Freelancer incidence is not converted into cash cost or pass-through; the missing target accounting basis is preserved.

Reviewed null low/base/high. The DEAL_EXECUTION type, closed five-year horizon, maximum/non-duplicative count calculation, and exact buyer/geography/archetype scope are preserved separately from onboarding.

Reviewed null low/base/high. With no normalized state or anchor support, the state digest and exit multiple remain missing; the closed exclusion map prevents duplication of C, survival, V, B, h, or sponsor effects.
Reviewed null low/base/high and null cost ID. The contaminated 5419 veterinary occupation proxy is not substituted, and no employee cost is double-counted as pass-through.
Reviewed null low/base/high. Broad provider sale intentions are correctly rejected as a defined U.S. target-band five-year offer rate and kept separate from access/win and price.
Up-front investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. No unsupported implementation or change cost is hidden in realization or commercial retention.
Year-one investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE and remain a separate nonnegative k schedule.
Year-two investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE and are not conflated with realization or retention.
Year-three investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE and retain the prescribed fraction-of-G unit.
Year-four investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE; the absence of evidence is not converted to a neutral zero base.
Year-five investment low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE; deterministic envelope extrema do not become claimed evidence.
Year-one realization low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE. No target adoption cohort or dated endpoint is inferred from sector AI commentary.
Year-two realization low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE and remain separate from k investment and c retention.
Year-three realization low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE; no neutral or monotonic ramp is invented.
Year-four realization low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE and the score envelope is not presented as a measured schedule.
Year-five realization low/base/high are null under MISSING/ESTIMATE/NONE; realization is not conflated with operator survival.

Reviewed null low/base/high. The INTEGRATION_ONBOARDING type, five-year completion requirements, maximum/non-duplicative count, and exact buyer/geography/archetype scope are preserved separately from deal execution.
Reviewed the single missing low/base/high state. All six dimensions, state ID, and digest remain null while entry-equivalence policy stays frozen; no alternative anchor channel is introduced.

Reviewed null low/base/high and empty basket under MISSING. The exact fixed-base Laspeyres assertions remain present, but no baseline service, new demand, repricing, implementation, or valuation is fabricated.

Reviewed null low/base/high. The exact accrual/TTM/GROSS_OF_ALL basis is present; no pass-through IDs exist, both disjoint partitions are empty, and missing construct evidence prevents an invented R_cva or G.
Recomputed 7/28=0.25, 14/28=0.50, and 21/28=0.75. PROXY/LOW is honest for the non-random provider list and assumed EBITDA-band denominator; no industry-wide inference is made.

findings.explanation: The frozen A2 metadata calls the cited endpoint a 2017 NAICS archive, but the reopened Census page labels itself 1997 NAICS. The displayed 541930 definition still supports the modeled translation/interpreting scope, so this is a source-vintage labeling caveat rather than an economic or range defect.
findings.remediation: Correct A2's vintage label or replace its URL with the intended 2017 archive in a future frozen specification; do not change current count arithmetic solely because of this label mismatch.
findings.publication_caveat: A2 is labeled as a 2017 NAICS archive in the frozen specification, but the cited endpoint identifies itself as the 1997 NAICS archive; the displayed 541930 scope remains consistent with the modeled translation/interpreting boundary.

publication_caveats: inputs.buy_mult: Do not infer a multiple from ranking revenue or M&A intentions.
inputs.buyer_access_win_share: Access/win must remain separate from sale availability and price.
inputs.controllable_contractor_cash_cost: Freelancer prevalence does not identify controllable cash cost or pass-through treatment.
inputs.employee_cash_cost: Do not use the contaminated broad-industry occupation mix.
inputs.five_year_sale_availability: Broad stated M&A intentions are not sale availability.
inputs.normalized_y5_operating_state: Without a state, no state digest or entry-equivalent anchor can be created.
inputs.recognized_revenue: The frozen specification forbids inventing a controllable value-added denominator.
inputs.target_archetype_coverage: The ranked cohort is non-random, contains verified and estimated revenue, and does not prove NAICS assignment or EBITDA for each provider.
inputs.target_archetype_coverage: Classification uncertainty remains because the frozen translation/localization and interpreting ranges overlap.
industry_spec.value_basis: Do not use any veterinary occupation from the dataset's 4-digit 5419 OEWS mix.
industry_spec.value_basis: Do not treat employee payroll share as total controllable cash cost in a contractor-intensive provider model.
industry_spec.value_basis: Do not infer revenue pass-through, controllable contractor cost, or mutually exclusive role weights from worker counts, task incidence, or broad-industry occupation shares.
industry_spec.value_basis: Selection uncertainty requires any future target-specific basis to encompass plausible reclassification to the overlapping interpreting-led archetype.

A2 is labeled as a 2017 NAICS archive in the frozen specification, but the cited endpoint identifies itself as the 1997 NAICS archive; the displayed 541930 scope remains consistent with the modeled translation/interpreting boundary.
summary: Publishable with caveats. The frozen inputs, packet, record, memo, receipts, arithmetic, causal separations, score envelopes, gates, and UNPROVEN/EVIDENCE_FIRST result reproduce exactly. Target-specific economics remain honestly missing rather than being scored as measured weakness. The only new caveat is A2's archive-vintage label mismatch, which does not change the displayed 541930 scope or the frozen proxy arithmetic.
